summary: Apollo Peptide Sciences sells third-party-tested, research-grade peptides and chemicals in lyophilized powder form, direct-to-consumer via e-commerce (23+ SKUs including BPC-157, GHK-Cu, NAD+, Semaglutide, Tirzepatide, and Retatrutide). It serves independent researchers, academic and institutional lab professionals, and scientifically-literate biohackers who need HPLC-verified purity (≥98%) with transparent Certificates of Analysis. All products are sold strictly for laboratory research use, not human consumption.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Convert a new newsletter subscriber into a first-time buyer by delivering the discount, building immediate trust around purity, and pre-selling the COA 'aha' moment.
campaigns.trigger: Newsletter signup / list join (send within seconds of subscribing).
campaigns.segment: New subscribers who have not yet placed a first order.
campaigns.rationale: Apollo's only entry offer today is a bare 10%-off code, and trust (verifiable purity) is the single biggest barrier to a first order — the welcome send is where the discount and the COA proof story must arrive together to earn that first purchase.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, deliver the 10% code + free-shipping unlock, and frame the COA transparency that proves purity before the buyer ever pays.
emails.subject: Welcome to Apollo — your 10% code + the COA behind every vial
emails.preview: Your discount's inside. So is the third-party HPLC data that backs every peptide we ship.
keyPoints: Deliver the 10% storewide code and confirm free shipping unlocks at $200
Lead with the trust hook: every SKU ships with an independent Certificate of Analysis verifying ≥98% (often ≥99%) HPLC purity
Set expectations — a short series ahead covering how to read a COA and how to store lyophilized vials
Include the required 'For research purposes only — not for human consumption' disclaimer and a clear opt-out

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move trust-hesitant subscribers toward a first purchase by teaching them to verify purity for themselves — turning Apollo's COA transparency into a competitive proof point.
campaigns.trigger: Subscriber joins the list but does not purchase; enters educational nurture track.
campaigns.segment: Non-purchasing subscribers below the buy-ready threshold; branch by peptide interest (longevity vs. metabolic GLP class).
campaigns.rationale: The #1 objection is 'is the purity actually what they claim?' — an educational sequence that teaches researchers how to read a real COA converts the skepticism that kills first orders into the exact reason Apollo wins.

emails.purpose: Evaluation-stage proof email: teach the buyer how to read a peptide COA and what separates a documented vendor from one hiding behind a label.
emails.subject: How to read a peptide COA — and spot the vendors hiding one
emails.preview: HPLC, mass spec, and the three numbers that actually decide your results.
keyPoints: Walk through the anatomy of a Certificate of Analysis: HPLC purity %, mass-spec identity confirmation, and the issuing independent lab
Contrast transparent documentation against the murky, COA-free sourcing common in the space
Link directly to a live Apollo COA so the reader can verify a real product spec themselves
Frame strictly as research-input quality (no therapeutic or dosing language) and include the research-only disclaimer

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate one-time buyers who never reordered — the largest recoverable revenue pool given there is no subscription lock-in.
campaigns.trigger: No purchase or engagement in 60–90 days.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed buyers (purchased once, dormant); branch separately from never-purchased dormant subscribers.
campaigns.rationale: With no subscription or loyalty hook, Apollo's default is single-transaction relationships, so lapsed buyers quietly pile up — a win-back is fast to ship and targets the biggest recoverable revenue with people who already trust the purity.

emails.purpose: Remind a lapsed researcher of Apollo's core value (verified purity at a fair price) and surface what's new since their last order to pull them back to the bench.
emails.subject: Still running studies? Here's what's new on the bench
emails.preview: New SKUs, fresh COAs, and your subscriber discount still works.
keyPoints: Open with the relationship, not a guilt trip: 'it's been a while — your research may have moved on'
Surface what's new since they left: added SKUs (e.g., Retatrutide), fresh batch COAs, and continued ≥98% HPLC verification
Reinstate a reason to return — reactivate their discount and note free shipping over $200
Include the research-only disclaimer and a clear opt-out / sunset path for non-responders

campaigns.name: Reorder & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Drive repeat purchase before a researcher's supply runs out, and grow order value through multi-pack and complementary-peptide expansion.
campaigns.trigger: Estimated reorder window (~30–90 days after last purchase) based on product type and quantity.
campaigns.segment: Active past buyers approaching their likely restock point; branch by what they bought (single vial vs. bulk; longevity vs. GLP class).
campaigns.rationale: Reconstituted peptides have a short usable window and lyophilized stock depletes as studies continue, so a timed reorder nudge captures predictable repeat revenue that Apollo currently leaves to chance with no reorder flow in place.

emails.purpose: Prompt a timely reorder and lift AOV by surfacing bulk savings and complementary peptides as research scope expands.
emails.subject: Running low? Reorder before your study stalls
emails.preview: Lyophilized vials keep for months — reconstituted, only weeks. Time to restock.
keyPoints: Anchor the timing to research reality: reconstituted material has a limited usable window, so restocking ahead avoids a stalled study
Offer the expansion path — multi-packs (Box of 10, 5-pack) and bulk vial sizes for volume savings
Cross-sell complementary peptides relevant to their last order and nudge toward the $200 free-shipping threshold
Keep framing strictly as research supply; include the research-only disclaimer and opt-out

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long research cycle and reinforce Apollo's authority on purity and sourcing — without discounting.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding and route recently-dormant contacts to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: Repeat purchase here is driven by ongoing research needs rather than a subscription, so an always-on authority newsletter keeps Apollo the default trusted supplier between orders and feeds both reorders and expansion.

emails.purpose: Establish the recurring flagship issue that delivers one useful, on-brand research idea and a soft path back to the catalog.
emails.subject: The Apollo Bench: what ≥99% purity actually changes in vitro
emails.preview: This month's purity breakdown, plus a freshly published Retatrutide COA.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send — this issue: why HPLC purity above 99% matters for reproducible in-vitro work
Recurring habit section ('The Apollo Bench') with a new batch COA spotlight each month
80% value / 20% soft offer — a single primary CTA back to the relevant catalog page, no discount push
Maintain scientific, non-therapeutic framing; include the research-only disclaimer and opt-out
